title: PerVendorFindingsFile
description: Schema for findings-{vendor}-{review_type}.json files. The file is a wrapper object containing review metadata and a findings array. This shape is what the existing review_dispatcher.py writes and what consensus_synthesizer.py reads (data.get('findings', [])). This proposal does NOT change the per-vendor file shape; the schema documents it.

reviewer_vendor.description: Vendor identifier producing this file. Pattern enforced for path safety (no separators, no spaces).

items.$comment: Matches openspec/schemas/review-findings.schema.json — vendor identity comes from the wrapper's reviewer_vendor field, not from each individual finding. Per-finding 'vendor' is OPTIONAL (some emitters include it for cross-vendor consensus matching; others rely on the wrapper). Listing it as required would reject existing valid vendor outputs.

id.description: Vendor-scoped unique identifier. Integer or string.

criticality.description: Matches the existing review-findings.schema.json enum and consensus_synthesizer.py rankings. The synthesizer's _higher_criticality() expects this set; using 'blocking' instead would break ranking.

line_range.description: Line range. Currently typed permissively to tolerate vendors that emit either {start,end} or 'N-M' string. The latter shape causes the consensus_synthesizer.py:59 bug — out of scope here, addressed by separate proposal.

vendor.description: Vendor identifier matching the file's filename component. Pattern enforced for path safety.
